source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Two electric dipole oscillators vibrate with the same frequency $\omega$, but their phases differ by $\frac{\pi}{2}$. The amplitudes of the dipole moments are both equal to $\mathbf{P}_{0}$, but the two vectors are at an angle $\psi_{0}$ to each other, (let $\mathbf{P}_{1}$ be along the $x$-axis and $\mathbf{P}_{2}$ in the $xy$ plane) as in Fig. 4.36. For an oscillating dipole $\mathbf{P}$ at the origin, the $\mathbf{B}$-field in the radiation zone is given by $\mathbf{B}=k^{2} \frac{1}{r} e^{i k r}\left(\frac{\mathbf{r}}{r} \times \mathbf{P}\right)$. Find the average angular distribution.

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: Two electric dipole oscillators vibrate with the same frequency $\omega$, but their phases differ by $\frac{\pi}{2}$. The amplitudes of the dipole moments are both equal to $\mathbf{P}_{0}$, and the two vectors are at an angle $\psi_{0}$ to each other. Let $\mathbf{P}_{1}$ be along the $x$-axis and $\mathbf{P}_{2}$ lie in the $xy$ plane. For an oscillating dipole $\mathbf{P}$ at the origin, the $\mathbf{B}$-field in the radiation zone is given by $\mathbf{B}=k^{2} \frac{1}{r} e^{i k r}\left(\frac{\mathbf{r}}{r} \times \mathbf{P}\right)$. Find the average angular distribution.

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Two electric dipole oscillators vibrate with the same frequency $\omega$, but their phases differ by $\frac{\pi}{2}$. The amplitudes of the dipole moments are both equal to $\mathbf{P}_{0}$, but the two vectors are at an angle $\psi_{0}$ to each other, (let $\mathbf{P}_{1}$ be along the $x$-axis and $\mathbf{P}_{2}$ in the $xy$ plane) as in Fig. 4.36. For an oscillating dipole $\mathbf{P}$ at the origin, the $\mathbf{B}$-field in the radiation zone is given by $\mathbf{B}=k^{2} \frac{1}{r} e^{i k r}\left(\frac{\mathbf{r}}{r} \times \mathbf{P}\right)$. Find the average angular distribution.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

metadata.reasoning_chain: The electric dipole moments of the two oscillators are $\mathbf{P}_{1}=P_{0} e^{-i \omega t} \mathbf{e}_{x}$ and $\mathbf{P}_{2}=P_{0}(\cos \psi_{0} \mathbf{e}_{x}+\sin \psi_{0} \mathbf{e}_{y}) e^{-i(\omega t-\frac{\pi}{2})}$. The dipole moment of the whole system is $\mathbf{P}=\mathbf{P}_{1}+\mathbf{P}_{2}$ and the magnetic field in Gaussian units is given by $\mathbf{B}=k^{2} \frac{1}{r} e^{i k r}(\mathbf{e}_{r} \times \mathbf{P})$. Using $\mathbf{e}_{x}=\mathbf{e}_{r}\sin\theta\cos\varphi+\mathbf{e}_{\theta}\cos\theta\cos\varphi-\mathbf{e}_{\varphi}\sin\varphi$, $\mathbf{e}_{y}=\mathbf{e}_{r}\sin\theta\sin\varphi+\mathbf{e}_{\theta}\cos\theta\sin\varphi+\mathbf{e}_{\varphi}\cos\varphi$, and $e^{i\frac{\pi}{2}}=i$, we obtain $\mathbf{e}_{r}\times\mathbf{P}_{1}=P_{0}e^{-i \omega t}(\mathbf{e}_{\varphi}\cos\theta\cos\varphi+\mathbf{e}_{\theta}\sin\varphi)$ and $\mathbf{e}_{r}\times\mathbf{P}_{2}=iP_{0}e^{-i \omega t}[\mathbf{e}_{\varphi}\cos(\varphi-\psi_{0})\cos\theta+\mathbf{e}_{\theta}\sin(\varphi-\psi_{0})]$. Therefore, the magnetic field becomes $\mathbf{B}=\frac{k^{2}P_{0}}{r}\left\{[\sin\varphi+i\sin(\varphi-\psi_{0})]\mathbf{e}_{\theta}+[\cos\varphi+i\cos(\varphi-\psi_{0})]\cos\theta\mathbf{e}_{\varphi}\right\}e^{i(kr-\omega t)}$. (a) The average power per unit solid angle is then calculated as $\frac{d\bar{S}}{d\Omega}=\frac{c}{4\pi}\mathbf{B}\cdot\mathbf{B}^{*}r^{2}=\frac{P_{0}^{2}k^{4}c}{8\pi}\left\{2-\sin^{2}\theta[\cos^{2}\varphi+\cos^{2}(\varphi-\psi_{0})]\right\}$.
